markdown:
#  Export a Project Budget in Portfolio Financials 
#####  Legacy
This information is intended for accounts with the [Portfolio Financials](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/portfolio-financials/) and [Capital Planning](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-what-is-capital-planning) products in Procore. Please reach out to your Procore point of contact for more information.
##  Background 
The Budget tab within the Cost Tracker allows you to build project budgets with variances and anticipated costs. There are two different budgeting methods that you can use in Portfolio Financials: a _broken down budget_ , which will allow you to assign budgets to individual cost items through the Cost Tracker, and an _overall (lump sum) budget_ , where you have one amount for the entire project. The current published budget can be exported to an Excel file from Portfolio Financials.
##  Things to Consider 
  * **Required User Permissions** :
    * 'Full Access' to the project or higher.


##  Steps 
  1. Navigate to the **Cost Tracker** section of the **Project Page**.
  2. Click the **Budget** tab.
  3. Click **Export**.  
![Export Budget](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/17744531ac5c9e68bcd12943c633f1d2/ff7fdd7b5854c55b3e749ff2d7cb9b91/export-budget.png?fm=webp)
  4. An Excel file will begin downloading to your computer.


## See Also

  * [Add a Budget to a Project in Portfolio Financials](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/portfolio-financials/tutorials/add-a-budget-to-a-project-in-portfolio-financials)
  * [Break Down an Overall Project Budget in Portfolio Financials](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/portfolio-financials/tutorials/break-down-an-overall-project-budget-in-portfolio-financials)
title: Export a Project Budget in Portfolio Financials
description: How to export the budget of a Portfolio Financials project to an Excel file.
